rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America, Llc¿S Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 28, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m. Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC¿s demurrer will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s special demurrers to the third (false representation), fourth (intentional tort), sixth (negligence ¿ vehicle, clutch and brake components) and seventh (strict liability ¿ vehicle, clutch and brake components) causes of action of plaintiffs¿ complaint are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ complaint contains sufficient allegations to plead those causes of action. Prevailing party to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America, Llc¿S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs¿ Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 28, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m. Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ complaint will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion is denied. As to punitive damages, plaintiffs sufficiently pled that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression. As to the sixth and seventh causes of action, defendant¿s motion is denied as moot based on the ruling on defendant¿s demurrer regarding those two causes of action. Prevailing party to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

Defendant¿s special demurrers to the third (false representation), fourth (intentional tort), sixth (negligence ¿ vehicle, clutch and brake components) and seventh (strict liability ¿ vehicle, clutch and brake components) causes of action of plaintiffs¿ complaint are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ complaint contains sufficient allegations to plead those causes of action. Prevailing party to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jaguar Land Rover Of North America, Llc'S Motion To Strike The Punitive Damages Prayer And Allegations And The Sixth And Seventh Causes Of Action From Plaintiffs' Complaint For Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 28, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ complaint will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion is denied. As to punitive damages, plaintiffs sufficiently pled that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression. As to the sixth and seventh causes of action, defendant¿s motion is denied as moot based on the ruling on defendant¿s demurrer regarding those two causes of action. Prevailing party to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM M. MCRAY et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant The Dow Chemical Co.¿s special demurrers to the first (negligence), second (strict liability), third (false representation), fourth (intentional failure to warn), fifth (premises liability) and sixth (loss of consortium) causes of action in plaintiffs¿ complaint are sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. Additionally, defendant¿s special demurrer to the fifth cause of action for premises liability is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity to clarify whether they are alleging this cause of action against defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recover Unreimbursed Costs And Fees Against Quintrall Associates And Their Client Helmer Truck Parts, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Berry & Berry¿s motion to recover unreimbursed costs and fees against Quintrall & Associates and their client Helmer Truck Parts, Inc. is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)
